title: Incremental TF-IDF Index
implementationNotes: Incremental methods implemented as addDocument(), removeDocument(), updateDocument(). TFIDFEventSync class created for automatic event-based index updates.
impact: 5-10x faster ranked search updates by avoiding full index rebuilds on entity changes

singleEntityUpdate.current: O(n) full index rebuild on any change
singleEntityUpdate.target: O(t) incremental update for single entity (t = token count)

tasks.title: Add Incremental Update Methods to TFIDFIndexManager
tasks.description: Add methods for single-entity add/update/remove that update the TF-IDF index incrementally without full rebuild. Also add IDF recalculation optimization.
tasks.implementationNotes: Implemented as addDocument(), removeDocument(), updateDocument() with isInitialized() and getDocumentCount() helpers. Method names differ from spec but functionality matches.

implementation.purpose: Enable O(t) incremental index updates instead of O(n) full rebuilds (t = token count in entity). When an entity is created, updated, or deleted, only that entity's document vector needs updating, and IDF values only need recalculation for affected terms.
keyDecisions: addEntity() - add document vector for new entity
removeEntity() - remove document vector and update IDF
IDF recalculation is deferred (only recalc on next search if dirty flag set)
Maintain 'dirtyTerms' Set to track which terms need IDF recalc
implementation.existingCodeContext: TFIDFIndexManager.ts has buildIndex(), updateIndex(), loadIndex(), saveIndex(), getIndex(), clearIndex(), needsRebuild() methods. Add incremental methods after updateIndex().

stepByStep.action: Add dirty tracking property after the index property (around line 34)
Add addEntity method after updateIndex() method (around line 163)
Add hasDirtyTerms method for checking if recalc needed
Add Entity import at the top of the file
stepByStep.details:
Find the import line (around line 12) and update to:
import type { TFIDFIndex, DocumentVector, KnowledgeGraph, ReadonlyKnowledgeGraph, Entity } from '../types/index.js';

stepByStep.action: Update buildIndex() to initialize documentCount (around line 91)
Update loadIndex() to initialize documentCount (around line 186)

acceptanceCriteria: addEntity() method adds single entity to index in O(t) time (t = token count)
removeEntity() method removes entity from index in O(t) time (t = token count)
updateEntityInIndex() combines remove + add atomically
dirtyTerms Set tracks terms needing IDF recalc
recalculateDirtyIDF() updates only dirty terms' IDF values
hasDirtyTerms() returns true when recalc needed
getDirtyTermCount() returns count of dirty terms
buildIndex() and loadIndex() initialize documentCount and clear dirty set
Entity type imported from types
npm run typecheck passes
All existing TFIDFIndexManager tests pass

tasks.title: Hook TFIDFIndexManager to Graph Events
tasks.description: Subscribe TFIDFIndexManager to graph change events for automatic incremental index updates when entities are created, updated, or deleted.
tasks.implementationNotes: TFIDFEventSync class created in src/search/TFIDFEventSync.ts with enable()/disable()/isEnabled() methods for event subscription.

implementation.purpose: Connect the TFIDFIndexManager to the GraphEventEmitter so that the search index is automatically updated when entities change, without requiring manual rebuild.
keyDecisions: Subscription happens when RankedSearch is constructed
Listen to entityCreated, entityUpdated, entityDeleted events
Lazy IDF recalc - set dirty flag, recalc before next search
Must handle case where events are not available (backward compat)
implementation.existingCodeContext: RankedSearch.ts uses TFIDFIndexManager internally. Modify constructor to subscribe to events.

stepByStep.details:
Find the imports at the top and add:
import type { GraphEventEmitter, EntityCreatedEvent, EntityUpdatedEvent, EntityDeletedEvent } from '../core/GraphEventEmitter.js';

stepByStep.action: Add optional events parameter to constructor
Add event subscription in constructor after existing initialization
Add subscribeToEvents private method at the end of the class
Update the search method to recalculate dirty IDF before searching

acceptanceCriteria: RankedSearch constructor accepts optional events parameter
Subscribes to entityCreated, entityUpdated, entityDeleted events
entityCreated triggers addEntity() on TFIDFIndexManager
entityUpdated triggers updateEntityInIndex() on TFIDFIndexManager
entityDeleted triggers removeEntity() on TFIDFIndexManager
search() recalculates dirty IDF before executing search
Works without events (backward compatible)
npm run typecheck passes
All existing RankedSearch tests pass

tasks.title: Create Incremental TF-IDF Tests
tasks.description: Create comprehensive unit tests for the incremental TF-IDF update methods and integration tests for automatic event-driven updates.
tasks.implementationNotes: Tests created in tests/unit/search/IncrementalTFIDF.test.ts covering addDocument, removeDocument, updateDocument, and TFIDFEventSync integration.

implementation.purpose: Verify incremental index updates work correctly, dirty term tracking is accurate, and event-driven updates maintain index consistency.
keyDecisions: Test each incremental method in isolation
Test dirty term tracking and lazy recalc
Test event-driven updates with mock emitter
Performance comparison: incremental vs full rebuild

stepByStep.details: Ensure all describe blocks are properly closed

acceptanceCriteria: Test file created at tests/unit/search/IncrementalTFIDF.test.ts
Tests for addEntity - success, duplicate prevention, no index case
Tests for removeEntity - success, non-existent, dirty marking
Tests for updateEntityInIndex - update existing, add new
Tests for recalculateDirtyIDF - recalc only dirty, IDF changes, term removal
Tests for edge cases - empty index, all removed
Performance test comparing incremental vs full rebuild
All tests pass

successCriteria: All three tasks completed with passing tests
addEntity(), removeEntity(), updateEntityInIndex() methods added to TFIDFIndexManager
Dirty term tracking with lazy IDF recalculation
RankedSearch subscribes to graph events for automatic updates
IDF recalculated before search when dirty terms exist
Incremental updates faster than full rebuild (performance test)
dependencies: Sprint 10.2 (Graph Change Events - for event subscription)
notes: CRITICAL: Dirty IDF recalculation is LAZY - only happens before next search
This avoids overhead when multiple entities are updated in sequence (e.g., import)
RankedSearch must be constructed with events parameter to enable auto-updates
Backward compatible - works without events (manual rebuild still supported)
Performance gain is most significant for large graphs with frequent small updates
